findings.title: __import__ in SANDBOX_BUILTINS enables dynamic forbidden-module loading, bypassing Stage 1 AST check
findings.description: sandbox_builtins.py line 29 includes the live __import__ callable. Stage 1 AST checker (syntax_checker.py:52-57) catches direct ast.Name references to __import__, but once the sandbox injects the live callable via module.__builtins__ = _SANDBOX_BUILTINS (sandbox_runner.py:739), strategy code can alias it (f = __import__) and call f(chr(111)+chr(115)). The ast.Name for the alias is not __import__, so Stage 1 passes. All FORBIDDEN_MODULES are reachable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace raw __import__ with a filtered wrapper that checks name.split(chr(46))[0] against FORBIDDEN_MODULES before forwarding.

findings.title: Memory monitor uses whole-process RSS without baseline; causes false positives in long atlas-loops
findings.description: _SandboxWorker._mem_poll() lines 103-122 compares proc.memory_info().rss directly against _MEM_LIMIT_BYTES=512MB with no baseline snapshot. In a long atlas-loop, prior-generation residual memory accumulates. If process RSS baseline already exceeds 512MB from prior generations, every subsequent valid strategy is immediately rejected. __init__ does not record baseline_rss.
findings.suggested_fix: In __init__ add: self._baseline_rss = psutil.Process().memory_info().rss. In _mem_poll check: if (rss - self._baseline_rss) > _MEM_LIMIT_BYTES.

findings.title: Stage 3 exception threshold is absolute count (4 allowed) not rate-based; logic-broken strategies may pass
findings.description: run_sandbox() lines 389-407 accumulates exceptions and only fails at count >= 5. A strategy crashing 4/200 bars (2%) receives Stage 3 PASS with WARNING (lines 420-423). No rate-based gate exists. TypeError and AttributeError indicate deterministic code bugs and should trigger immediate FAIL.
findings.suggested_fix: After the loop compute exception_rate = len(result.exceptions_caught) / bars_tested. If > 0.01, set result.passed=False. Immediately fail on TypeError or AttributeError regardless of count.

findings.title: _SimpleFeatureStore NaN fallback on compute error is invisible to run_layer1; broken strategy may reach Layer 2
findings.description: vectorbt_engine.py lines 362-376: on any non-ValueError exception from an indicator, the code logs an error, fills the feature with NaN, appends to _feature_compute_errors, and continues. run_layer1 never inspects _feature_compute_errors after _generate_signals_vectorized (lines 785-791). Strategy runs on NaN features, may meet lenient L1 thresholds, and proceed to Layer 2 as fundamentally broken.
findings.suggested_fix: After _generate_signals_vectorized in run_layer1, add: if getattr(feature_store, "_feature_compute_errors", None): return Layer1Result(passed=False, error=str(feature_store._feature_compute_errors)).

findings.title: L2 sensitivity trial failures at DEBUG level only; cost-stress failures lose traceback
findings.description: _run_l2_param_sensitivity inner loop lines 1221-1223 uses logger.debug. With default INFO level, all trial failures are silent. When all trials fail the function returns None and Gate fails with no diagnostic. _run_cost_sensitivity_bt lines 1136-1153 uses logger.warning without exc_info=True, losing the stack trace.
findings.suggested_fix: Line 1222: raise to logger.warning with exc_info=True. Lines 1137 and 1152: add exc_info=True.

findings.title: TradeSignal and StrategyConfig lack extra=forbid; extra fields from generated code silently accepted
findings.description: atlas/common/models.py: TradeSignal line 62, StrategyConfig line 84, and FillEvent line 123 use Pydantic v2 default extra=ignore. Generated strategy code is untrusted. A generate_signal() returning TradeSignal with extra fields validates without error. CLAUDE.md mandates thorough Pydantic I/O validation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid") to TradeSignal and StrategyConfig. FillEvent may use extra="ignore" for dict compatibility.

findings.title: equity[0]<=0 fallback (total_return_pct=0.0) can make Gate #13 B&H excess return incorrectly PASS
findings.description: _compute_metrics lines 831-840: when equity[0]<=0, total_return_pct=0.0 is set with a warning. bh_excess_return_pct (lines 875-881) then computes: 0.0 - bh_return_pct * exposure. In a bear-market period where bh_return_pct is negative (e.g. -10%, exposure=0.3), result is +3.0, passing Gate #13 (>= 0.0). A corrupted BT thus falsely generates apparent alpha.
findings.suggested_fix: When equity[0]<=0, set total_return_pct=None and bh_excess_return_pct=None. The _check() helper returns False for None, Gate #13 correctly fails.
